functions.name: azureml.designer.modules.recommendation.dnn.common.tf_feature_columns.is_basic_feature
documentation:
Check if a feature column represents the basic feature.

A basic feature is the feature existed in the input feature dataset, and non-basic feature is the feature produced
based on basic features with feature engineering, such as crossed features, this kind of features are not existed
in the input dataset.

functions.name: azureml.designer.modules.recommendation.dnn.common.tf_feature_columns.parse_basic_features
documentation:
Find all unique basic features in the feature columns.

Even there are not any basic feature in feature_columns, the basic features can be returned, because some non-basic
features would rely on the basic features.

classes.name: azureml.designer.modules.recommendation.dnn.common.tf_feature_columns.FeatureColumn

methods.name: azureml.designer.modules.recommendation.dnn.common.tf_feature_columns.FeatureColumn.reset
documentation: This method would be used to reset _feature_column attr to be None, usually is called before model saving.

This is a base class for tensorflow feature columns wrapper classes.

The main function for these classes is to record feature column build info. And after model loading, the
original feature columns can be rebuilt.

classes.name: azureml.designer.modules.recommendation.dnn.common.feature_builder.FeatureMeta
documentation:
The tool class recorded feature metas.

Feature meta includes four fields: feature original name, feature type, values used to fill missing elements,
and the value vocabulary for categorical feature.

classes.name: azureml.designer.modules.recommendation.dnn.common.feature_builder.FeatureBuilder

methods.name: azureml.designer.modules.recommendation.dnn.common.feature_builder.FeatureBuilder.__init__
documentation:
Init ids and features.

This function would record ids and features values. And feature metas will also recorded, which can be checked
in the update method.

```
param ids

represents feature identifiers with pd.Series type

param id_key

the key represent id feature

param features

FeatureDataset type, where the first column must be feature identifiers, and the remain
```

columns are features
\:param feat_key_suffix: when rename features, this string will be append the new names, this param is useful
when there will be many feature builders and to avoid duplicate feature keys

methods.name: azureml.designer.modules.recommendation.dnn.common.feature_builder.FeatureBuilder.build_feature_lookup
documentation:
Build feature lookup for the given ids.

With ids provided, the module checks \_features_df attr, and finds corresponding features for those ids.
If \_features_df is None, then only convert ids to string type and return.
\:param ids: the identifiers to built with features
\:return: pd.DataFrame, feature lookup for ids, the dataframe indexes are the given ids.

methods.name: azureml.designer.modules.recommendation.dnn.common.feature_builder.FeatureBuilder.update
documentation:
Update existing features with the new features dataset.

The method checks the compatibility between existing features and new features dataset. Then update
unseen identifiers features according to new features. The features for existing identifiers would not be
updated.
\:param features: FeatureDataset, the new features dataset provided to update the existing features.

methods.name: azureml.designer.modules.recommendation.dnn.common.feature_builder.FeatureBuilder._check_features
documentation:
Check compatibility between recorded features and the given features.

The two feature dataset are compatibility if:
1. The new feature dataset contains all feature names in the old feature dataset
2. The same feature in two dataset is of same type

methods.name: azureml.designer.modules.recommendation.dnn.common.feature_builder.FeatureBuilder._init_feature_meta
documentation:
Init feature meta for the feature, which is described as pd.Series type.

The DateTime, TimeSpan and NAN type features would be ignored. And the numeric features with NAN mean
value is also ignored.

The tool class helps recommenders to build training/test dataset with normalized features.

The class has three main functionality:
1. Record (user/item) ids vocab in id_vocab attribute. This attr is usually init during training stage and re-used
in score stage.
2. Record features in _features_df and _features_meta attributes. These attrs would be used to build dataset with
features, which is usually used in hybrid recommendation.
3. Provide update method to update features for unseen ids during training.

methods.name: azureml.designer.modules.recommendation.dnn.common.entry_param.EntryParam.load
documentation:
Try to load p_value to the specified p_type type.

If p_value is None or has already been of p_type, just return, or try to find loaders in params_loader.

modules.name: azureml.designer.modules.recommendation.dnn.common.score_column_names
documentation: common constant defined for three kinds of tasks

documentation: for DataFrameSchema score column name attr support

functions.name: azureml.designer.modules.recommendation.dnn.common.score_column_names.build_rated_ranking_column_name_keys
documentation: for DataFrameSchema score column name attr support

documentation: for DataFrameSchema score column name attr support

methods.name: azureml.designer.modules.recommendation.dnn.common.dataset.Dataset.is_all_na_column
documentation: Determine if a column has all nan values.

classes.name: azureml.designer.modules.recommendation.dnn.common.dataset.TransactionDataset
documentation:
The class describes transactions for recommendation task.

A valid transaction dataset should have at least two columns, where the first column represents user ids,
the second column represent item ids, and an optional rating column as the last column.

classes.name: azureml.designer.modules.recommendation.dnn.common.dataset.FeatureDataset
documentation:
The class describes feature dataset for recommendation task.

The feature column should have at least two columns, where the first column represents user/item ids, and
the remaining columns represents all features.

functions.name: azureml.designer.modules.recommendation.dnn.wide_and_deep.common.preprocess.preprocess_transactions
documentation:
Preprocess transaction dataset.

The preprocess including:
1. Drop instances with missing values
2. Convert user/item ids to string type

functions.name: azureml.designer.modules.recommendation.dnn.wide_and_deep.common.preprocess.preprocess_features
documentation:
Preprocess feature dataset.

The preprocess including:
1. Drop instances with missing values
2. Convert user/item ids to string type

methods.name: azureml.designer.modules.recommendation.dnn.wide_and_deep.common.wide_n_deep_model.WideNDeepModel.default_columns
documentation:
Define a default wide columns and deep columns scheme.

The wide columns include, users, items, users and items crossed features.
The deep columns include, user embeddings, item embeddings, other categorical feature embeddings
and numeric features.

methods.name: azureml.designer.modules.recommendation.dnn.wide_and_deep.common.wide_n_deep_model.WideNDeepModel.build_optimizer
documentation:
Build the optimizer through optimizer name.

These optimizers come from TensorFlow library, for an overview, please refer to:
https://www.tensorflow.org/api_docs/python/tf/keras/optimizers
experimental optimizers are graduated to stable; explicitly use legacy optimizers here.
https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/releases/tag/v2.10.0

methods.name: azureml.designer.modules.recommendation.dnn.wide_and_deep.common.wide_n_deep_model.WideNDeepModel.build_activation_fn
documentation:
Build the activation function through activation function name.

This activation functions come from TensorFlow library, for an overview, please refer to:
https://www.tensorflow.org/api_docs/python/tf/keras/activations
